heading: Change of nonimmigrant classification
label: 1258 Change of nonimmigrant classification
notes: Amendments2006— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), substituted “Secretary of Homeland Security” for “Attorney General”, inserted “(subject to subsection (b))” after “except” in introductory provisions, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 109–1621996—, in introductory provisions, inserted “and who is not inadmissible under (or whose inadmissibility under such section is waived under )” after “maintain that status”.Pub. L. 104–208, § 301(b)(2)section 1182(a)(9)(B)(i) of this titlesection 1182(a)(9)(B)(v) of this titlePar. (1). , made technical amendment to directory language of . See 1994 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 104–208, § 671(a)(2)Pub. L. 103–322, § 130003(b)(3)1994—Par. (1). , as amended by , substituted “(K), or (S)” for “or (K)”.Pub. L. 103–322, § 130003(b)(3)Pub. L. 104–208, § 671(a)(2)1986—Par. (4). added par. (4).Pub. L. 99–6031981— permitted certain exchange visitors who are not subject to a requirement of returning to their home countries for two years, or who have had such requirement waived, to adjust to a visitor or diplomat status, prohibited the adjustment of nonimmigrant status by fiancee or fiance nonimmigrants, and specifically precluded the change of status with respect to doctors who have entered the United States as exchange visitors for graduate medical training, even if they have received a waiver of the two-year foreign residence requirement.Pub. L. 97–1161961— inserted references to paragraph (15)(J) of in two places.Pub. L. 87–256section 1101(a) of this title

children.text: an alien classified as a nonimmigrant under subparagraph (C), (D), (K), or (S) of ,section 1101(a)(15) of this title

an alien classified as a nonimmigrant under subparagraph (J) of who came to the United States or acquired such classification in order to receive graduate medical education or training,section 1101(a)(15) of this title

an alien (other than an alien described in paragraph (2)) classified as a nonimmigrant under subparagraph (J) of who is subject to the two-year foreign residence requirement of and has not received a waiver thereof, unless such alien applies to have the alien’s classification changed from classification under subparagraph (J) of to a classification under subparagraph (A) or (G) of such section, andsection 1101(a)(15) of this titlesection 1182(e) of this titlesection 1101(a)(15) of this title
an alien admitted as a nonimmigrant visitor without a visa under section 1182() of this title or .lsection 1187 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: The exceptions specified in paragraphs (1) through (4) of subsection (a) shall not apply to a change of nonimmigrant classification to that of a nonimmigrant under subparagraph (T) or (U) of .section 1101(a)(15) of this title
